GET /accounts/{account_id}/workers/scrip.description: List of Worker Versions. The first version in the list is the latest version.

parameters.description: Only return versions that can be used in a deployment. Ignores pagination.
parameters.name: deployable

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

author_email.description: Email of the user who created the version.

author_id.description: Identifier of the user who created the version.

hasPreview.description: Whether the version can be previewed.

modified_on.description: When the version was last modified.

source.description: The source of the version upload.

handlers.description: The names of handlers exported as part of the default export.

last_deployed_from.description: The client most recently used to deploy this Worker.

named_handlers.description: Named exports, such as Durable Object class implementations and named entrypoints.

handlers.description: The names of handlers exported as part of the named export.

name.description: The name of the exported class or entrypoint.

compatibility_date.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

compatibility_flags.description: Flags that enable or disable certain features in the Workers runtime.

additionalProperties.description:
A single entry in the `exports` map, keyed by export name (a
`WorkerEntrypoint` class name, a Durable Object class name, or
`default` for the Worker's default export). Worker entrypoint
entries set `type: worker` and may carry `cache` configuration
for that entrypoint. Durable Object entries set
`type: durable-object` and carry additional provisioning fields.

allOf.description:
CacheW configuration for a single `type: worker` entrypoint.
When present it overrides the Worker's global
`cache_options.enabled` for that entrypoint, so a Worker can turn
caching on globally and opt one entrypoint out, or the reverse.

enabled.description: Whether caching is enabled for this entrypoint.

cache.description:
Cache override for this entrypoint. It applies only to
`type: worker` entries and overrides the Worker's global
`cache_options.enabled` for that entrypoint.

renamed_to.description:
Destination class name for a `state: renamed` tombstone. The
target must appear as a live (`created`) entry in the same
`exports` map. Write-only: never present in GET responses.

state.description:
Lifecycle state of the export entry. Defaults to `created`
(a normal, live export) when omitted.

`deleted`, `renamed`, and `transferred` are tombstones:
write-only lifecycle operations that retire, rename, or hand
off a provisioned Durable Object namespace. They are applied
at upload and are filtered out of GET responses, so a read
only ever returns `created` or `expecting-transfer`.

`expecting-transfer` is a live export whose data is being
received from another script via the two-phase transfer flow;
it carries `storage` and `transfer_from`.

storage.description:
Storage backend for a `type: durable-object` export. Required
for live Durable Object entries (`created` and
`expecting-transfer`). `sqlite` selects SQLite-backed storage;
`legacy-kv` selects the legacy key-value storage.

transfer_from.description:
Source script for a `state: expecting-transfer` entry. The
namespace on this script is materialised from the source
script's data via the pending-transfer flow. Present on reads
for `expecting-transfer` entries.

transferred_to.description:
Destination script for a `state: transferred` tombstone. Must
reference a script in the same account; cross-dispatch-namespace
transfers are rejected. Write-only: never present in GET
responses.

type.description: The kind of export.

allOf.description:
Declarative exports for the Worker, keyed by export name. Worker
entrypoint entries carry cache configuration for that entrypoint.
Durable Object entries are also configured here.

exports.description:
Declarative exports for this version, including
Durable Object classes (with their `storage`
backend) and named Worker entrypoints. Tombstoned
lifecycle entries are omitted, so only live exports
(`created` and `expecting-transfer`) are returned.

cpu_ms.description: The amount of CPU time this Worker can use in milliseconds.

migration_tag.description: The tag of the Durable Object migration that was most recently applied for this Worker.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

author_email.description: Email of the user who created the version.

author_id.description: Identifier of the user who created the version.

hasPreview.description: Whether the version can be previewed.

modified_on.description: When the version was last modified.

source.description: The source of the version upload.

POST /accounts/{account_id}/workers/scri.description: Upload a Worker Version without deploying to Cloudflare's network. You can find more about the multipart metadata on our docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/multipart-upload-metadata/.

parameters.description: When set to "strict", the upload will fail if any `inherit` type bindings cannot be resolved against the previous version of the Worker. Without this, unresolvable inherit bindings are silently dropped.
parameters.name: bindings_inherit

files.contentType: application/javascript+module, text/javascript+module, application/javascript, text/javascript, `text/x-python`, text/x-python-requirement, application/wasm, text/plain, application/octet-stream, application/source-map

files.description:
An array of modules (often JavaScript files) comprising a Worker script. At least one module must be present and referenced in the metadata as `main_module` or `body_part` by filename.
Possible Content-Type(s) are: `application/javascript+module`, `text/javascript+module`, `application/javascript`, `text/javascript`, `text/x-python`, `text/x-python-requirement`, `application/wasm`, `text/plain`, `application/octet-stream`, `application/source-map`.

metadata.description: JSON-encoded metadata about the uploaded parts and Worker configuration.

workers/message.description: Human-readable message about the version. Truncated to 1000 bytes if longer.

workers/tag.description: User-provided identifier for the version. Maximum 100 bytes.

bindings.description: List of bindings attached to a Worker. You can find more about bindings on our docs: https://developers.cloudflare.com/workers/configuration/multipart-upload-metadata/#bindings.

name.description: A JavaScript variable name for the binding.

network_id.description: Identifier of the network to bind to. Only "cf1:network" is currently supported. Mutually exclusive with tunnel_id.

tunnel_id.description: UUID of the Cloudflare Tunnel to bind to. Mutually exclusive with network_id.

type.description: The kind of resource that the binding provides.

cache_options.description:
Global CacheW configuration for the Worker. When caching is on,
the platform provisions a `cloudflare.app` zone for the Worker.
A `type: worker` entry in the `exports` map can override this
value for a single entrypoint.

cross_version_cache.description:
Whether cached responses are shared across Worker version
uploads. This is independent of `enabled`. It can stay true
while caching is off, so the preference survives turning
caching off and back on.

enabled.description: Whether caching is enabled for this Worker.

compatibility_date.description: Date indicating targeted support in the Workers runtime. Backwards incompatible fixes to the runtime following this date will not affect this Worker.

compatibility_flags.description: Flags that enable or disable certain features in the Workers runtime. Used to enable upcoming features or opt in or out of specific changes not included in a `compatibility_date`.

items.description: Flag that enables or disables a specific feature in the Workers runtime.

additionalProperties.description:
A single entry in the `exports` map, keyed by export name (a
`WorkerEntrypoint` class name, a Durable Object class name, or
`default` for the Worker's default export). Worker entrypoint
entries set `type: worker` and may carry `cache` configuration
for that entrypoint. Durable Object entries set
`type: durable-object` and carry additional provisioning fields.

allOf.description:
CacheW configuration for a single `type: worker` entrypoint.
When present it overrides the Worker's global
`cache_options.enabled` for that entrypoint, so a Worker can turn
caching on globally and opt one entrypoint out, or the reverse.

enabled.description: Whether caching is enabled for this entrypoint.

cache.description:
Cache override for this entrypoint. It applies only to
`type: worker` entries and overrides the Worker's global
`cache_options.enabled` for that entrypoint.

renamed_to.description:
Destination class name for a `state: renamed` tombstone. The
target must appear as a live (`created`) entry in the same
`exports` map. Write-only: never present in GET responses.

state.description:
Lifecycle state of the export entry. Defaults to `created`
(a normal, live export) when omitted.

`deleted`, `renamed`, and `transferred` are tombstones:
write-only lifecycle operations that retire, rename, or hand
off a provisioned Durable Object namespace. They are applied
at upload and are filtered out of GET responses, so a read
only ever returns `created` or `expecting-transfer`.

`expecting-transfer` is a live export whose data is being
received from another script via the two-phase transfer flow;
it carries `storage` and `transfer_from`.

storage.description:
Storage backend for a `type: durable-object` export. Required
for live Durable Object entries (`created` and
`expecting-transfer`). `sqlite` selects SQLite-backed storage;
`legacy-kv` selects the legacy key-value storage.

transfer_from.description:
Source script for a `state: expecting-transfer` entry. The
namespace on this script is materialised from the source
script's data via the pending-transfer flow. Present on reads
for `expecting-transfer` entries.

transferred_to.description:
Destination script for a `state: transferred` tombstone. Must
reference a script in the same account; cross-dispatch-namespace
transfers are rejected. Write-only: never present in GET
responses.

type.description: The kind of export.

allOf.description:
Declarative exports for the Worker, keyed by export name. Worker
entrypoint entries carry cache configuration for that entrypoint.
Durable Object entries are also configured here.

exports.description:
Declarative exports for this version. Worker
entrypoint entries (`type: worker`) carry cache
configuration for that entrypoint.

keep_bindings.description: List of binding types to keep from previous_upload.

main_module.description: Name of the uploaded file that contains the main module (e.g. the file exporting a `fetch` handler). Indicates a `module syntax` Worker, which is required for Version Upload.

package_dependencies.description:
The list of npm packages that were installed and used when this Worker
version was built.

installedVersion.description: The exact version that was resolved and installed by the package manager.

name.description: The npm package name.

packageJsonVersion.description: The version constraint as written in package.json.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.

author_email.description: Email of the user who created the version.

author_id.description: Identifier of the user who created the version.

hasPreview.description: Whether the version can be previewed.

modified_on.description: When the version was last modified.

source.description: The source of the version upload.

enabled.description: Whether caching is enabled for this entrypoint.

cache.description:

allOf.description:
Declarative exports for the Worker, keyed by export name. Worker
entrypoint entries carry cache configuration for that entrypoint.
Durable Object entries are also configured here.

exports.description:
Declarative exports for this version, including
Durable Object classes (with their `storage`
backend) and named Worker entrypoints. Tombstoned
lifecycle entries are omitted, so only live exports
(`created` and `expecting-transfer`) are returned.

cache.description:
Cache override for this entrypoint. It applies only to
`type: worker` entries and overrides the Worker's global
`cache_options.enabled` for that entrypoint.

renamed_to.description:
Destination class name for a `state: renamed` tombstone. The
target must appear as a live (`created`) entry in the same
`exports` map. Write-only: never present in GET responses.

state.description:
Lifecycle state of the export entry. Defaults to `created`
(a normal, live export) when omitted.

`deleted`, `renamed`, and `transferred` are tombstones:
write-only lifecycle operations that retire, rename, or hand
off a provisioned Durable Object namespace. They are applied
at upload and are filtered out of GET responses, so a read
only ever returns `created` or `expecting-transfer`.

`expecting-transfer` is a live export whose data is being
received from another script via the two-phase transfer flow;
it carries `storage` and `transfer_from`.

storage.description:
Storage backend for a `type: durable-object` export. Required
for live Durable Object entries (`created` and
`expecting-transfer`). `sqlite` selects SQLite-backed storage;
`legacy-kv` selects the legacy key-value storage.

transfer_from.description:
Source script for a `state: expecting-transfer` entry. The
namespace on this script is materialised from the source
script's data via the pending-transfer flow. Present on reads
for `expecting-transfer` entries.

transferred_to.description:
Destination script for a `state: transferred` tombstone. Must
reference a script in the same account; cross-dispatch-namespace
transfers are rejected. Write-only: never present in GET
responses.

type.description: The kind of export.

allOf.description:
Declarative exports for the Worker, keyed by export name. Worker
entrypoint entries carry cache configuration for that entrypoint.
Durable Object entries are also configured here.

exports.description:
Declarative exports for this version, including
Durable Object classes (with their `storage`
backend) and named Worker entrypoints. Tombstoned
lifecycle entries are omitted, so only live exports
(`created` and `expecting-transfer`) are returned.

cpu_ms.description: The amount of CPU time this Worker can use in milliseconds.

migration_tag.description: The tag of the Durable Object migration that was most recently applied for this Worker.

usage_model.description: Usage model for the Worker invocations.

success.description: Whether the API call was successful.
